description:
**Milestone:** Private Ava Channel

Three MCP tools: send_channel_message (post to private Ava channel), read_channel_messages (read with filters), file_system_improvement (create feature on System Improvements board from channel discussion). Create an ongoing:true 'System Improvements' project where Avas file self-improvement tickets when they observe recurring friction. Update /ava skill prompt with private channel instructions: this is YOUR space, no humans can write, use it to coordinate, discuss bugs, propose improvements. The flywheel: observe friction > discuss in channel > file ticket > auto-mode fixes it > friction reduced.

**Files to Modify:**
- packages/mcp-server/src/tools/ava-channel-tools.ts
- packages/mcp-server/src/tools/index.ts
- apps/server/src/routes/ava-channel/index.ts
- apps/server/src/server/routes.ts

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] send_channel_message MCP tool posts natural-language message to private Ava channel
- [ ] read_channel_messages MCP tool returns recent messages with optional time range and instance filter
- [ ] file_system_improvement MCP tool creates feature on System Improvements board from discussion context
- [ ] Server routes: POST /api/ava-channel/send, GET /api/ava-channel/messages
- [ ] Messages from MCP tools propagate via CRDT to all connected instances
- [ ] System Improvements project created as ongoing:true on first use
- [ ] /ava skill prompt updated with private channel section when hivemind active
- [ ] No forced posting cadence — Avas post when they have something to say
- [ ] Rate limit: max 3 system improvement tickets per instance per day
- [ ] Dedup check: search existing backlog before filing a system improvement ticket
- [ ] Minimum discussion threshold: at least 2 Ava instances must discuss a friction point before filing

**Complexity:** medium

**Guardrails:** If this phase involves new or changed behavior, include tests that verify correctness. If this phase adds, removes, or changes a user-facing feature, API endpoint, configuration option, or service, update the relevant documentation in `docs/` to reflect those changes.
